title: Week of March 24, 2026
summary: This week focused on improving platform reliability and making OpsPal easier to set up on Windows. We fixed multiple connection and data processing issues that were causing errors, and added native Windows support for automatic installation of required tools. These improvements make OpsPal more stable and accessible for teams working across different operating systems.

entries.headline: Fixed data sync errors between Salesforce and OpsPal
details: Resolved 6 connection issues that were blocking automatic data updates
Data flows now work more reliably during bulk operations
Added backup options when primary connections fail

entries.headline: Simple record lookups now work faster
details: Single record searches complete without unnecessary processing steps
Looking up individual records by ID now takes less time
Short queries with small result sets process more efficiently

entries.headline: Windows setup now installs required tools automatically
details: OpsPal setup automatically installs development tools on Windows
No more manual installation of Node.js and other dependencies
One-click installation process for Windows users

entries.headline: Better error detection and reporting for background tasks
details: OpsPal now catches connection problems earlier
Error messages include more helpful context about what went wrong
Background processes validate connections before starting work

entries.headline: Windows installation script works with browser-based setup
details: Fixed issues when running setup directly from browser
Installation no longer stops unexpectedly on Windows
Setup process handles different Windows configurations better

entries.headline: Salesforce data queries work better in production environments
details: Read-only data access now works smoothly in live Salesforce orgs
Reduced restrictions on viewing production data for analysis

entries.headline: Revenue analysis results save properly for future reference
details: Assessment findings now include complete revenue context
Historical analysis data persists between sessions

entries.headline: New customization system for templates and branding
details: Add custom templates and brand elements that survive updates
Customize OpsPal interface while keeping core functionality intact
Changes persist automatically across different workspaces
